about.title: About linux-meta
about.intro: Early-stage project (v0.1 · early access). We're building, in public, a metadata layer for Linux packages — age ratings, translations, privacy signals. Most of what exists today is redistribution of upstream data with canonicalization and preparation of complete snapshots for local reuse. Original project work is still small and documented below.
about.missionBody: Build an open metadata layer for the Linux ecosystem — starting with age ratings and translated descriptions. Everything CC0, auditable, consumable by any distribution.
about.valuesBody: Honesty about what we ship today · Provenance on every data point · Independence from companies and governments · Accessibility is mandatory · Privacy by design.
about.governanceBody: Independent project. The public governance page is live at /governanca and still evolving. Decisions are made by the maintainer with input from contributors on GitHub until the formal structure is complete.
about.originBody: Project started in 2026 out of a discussion about recent age-rating laws for digital content in several countries. Instead of waiting, we decided to build the missing open infrastructure. Work is still early-stage — collaboration and criticism welcome.
about.stateIntro: Exact metrics computed live from the database. We separate redistribution (imported) from project work (ai/human):
gapsList: We do not offer an API as a product. The focus is complete snapshots for download and local reuse.
No active human reviewers yet. All in-house classifications are AI-generated and pending review.
No financial report published yet. First report will be issued after the first effective semester of operation.
No formal partnerships with distributions, NGOs or public bodies. We are open to conversations.
No seal, certification or registered trademark. Everything we offer is the open catalog and public snapshots.

partners.title: Partners
partners.intro: We have no formal documented partnerships yet. This page exists as an invitation to interested distributions, NGOs and institutions.
partners.openBody: If you represent a Linux distribution that wants to reuse the complete dataset, an NGO working on online child safety, an educational institution or a public body interested in open standards: open an issue on GitHub or use the contact form.
partners.criteriaBody: We only partner with organizations that accept (1) public disclosure of the partnership with clear scope, (2) commitment to the open CC0 dataset, (3) formal conflict-of-interest declaration.

sponsor.title: Support the project
sponsor.intro: You can now support linux-meta through GitHub Sponsors. The commitment stays the same: transparency, no tiers, and no benefits beyond an optional public mention.
sponsor.supportBody: One-time or recurring contributions through GitHub Sponsors help cover infrastructure (server, domain) and the time spent curating the data.
sponsor.howBody: No tiers and no benefits that create paid priority — support does not buy influence over the data or moderation. The only return is an optional public mention. A revenue and expense report will be published periodically.
sponsor.interestBody: If you or your organization wants to arrange another form of support, open an issue on GitHub with the `funding` tag.

transparency.title: Transparency
transparency.intro: This page is the direct reflection of what the project ships — no makeup.
transparency.gapTitle: What we promise × What we ship today
gapItems: 0 published. First scheduled after first effective semester.
Not promised. The distribution model is a complete snapshot for download and local reuse.
~3,990 packages have at least one locale — 99% imported from the author's upstream AppStream metainfo. Only 18 strings generated by the project's AI. 0 translated by identified humans.
transparency.metricsTitle: Real metrics (live from the database, refreshed every minute)
transparency.metricsBody: Counters on the home page are split by origin (imported vs AI vs human) to avoid inflating original project work.

conduct.title: Code of Conduct
conduct.intro: Everyone who takes part in linux-meta — contributor, reviewer, maintainer — follows these principles:
rules: Treat people with respect regardless of background, gender, religion, political opinion or ability.
Criticize the work, never the person.
When you disagree, offer evidence. When you are wrong, say so.
Do not share personal data of third parties, including dispute reporters.
Do not use the project to push partisan agendas.
Report abuse via private GitHub Issues or direct contact with the maintainer (link on /about).
conduct.enforce: Violations are reviewed by the maintainer and may result in temporary suspension or permanent ban. A conduct committee structure will be formalized once there are ≥3 active maintainers.
